title: Path Traversal / Arbitrary File Read via isPathSafe
description: The `isPathSafe` utility explicitly allowed access to the project root directory. API endpoints (e.g., `/api/process`, `/api/drafts`) using this check could be manipulated to read or process arbitrary files in the project root (including source code and potential secrets), violating the security policy defined in AGENTS.md.
remediation: Modified `isPathSafe` to strictly allow only paths within the `stagingDir`. Removed `projectRoot` from the allowed prefix list.

title: Vulnerable Dependency (Next.js)
description: Next.js version 16.1.1 contains known high-severity vulnerabilities (GHSA-h25m-26qc-wcjf) related to HTTP request deserialization and DoS.

title: Arbitrary File Write / Upload to Gemini via Reprocess API
description: The `/api/process` endpoint allows specifying a local `filePath` for reprocessing. Combined with SEC-001, this could allow an attacker to upload arbitrary local files (e.g., source code) to Google Gemini or process them inline, leading to data exfiltration.

title: Missing CI/CD Security Gates
description: The `.github/workflows/ci.yml` pipeline runs tests and linting but lacks specific security checks such as SCA (`npm audit`), Secret Scanning, or blocking SAST.
remediation: Update CI workflow to include `npm audit` and a secret scanner.

title: TypeScript Compilation Failures
description: The codebase contained several TypeScript errors preventing a clean build, including missing properties in interfaces (`FFmpegConfig`) and invalid assignments to read-only properties (`NODE_ENV`).
remediation: Fixed type definitions, updated mock objects, and corrected test logic.

title: Linting Violations
description: Over 400 ESLint violations were detected, primarily `no-explicit-any` and `no-unused-vars`. While not immediate functional bugs, they indicate technical debt.
remediation: Run `npm run lint --fix` and schedule refactoring to remove `any` types.
